question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report was released today showing CPI increased 0.3% in March, well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. Since the official BLS data is now available and confirms CPI rose only 0.3%, this market should resolve NO with 100% certainty. The current 31% price appears to reflect outdated pre-data expectations rather than the actual reported outcome.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Market resolution is now deterministic based on published data
Energy price concerns from Iran conflict did not push CPI above threshold
risks: Possible data revision by BLS in future months
Market may not be fully updated with today's data release
